page: Amazon Web Services r6idn.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supporting advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. Memory capacity stands at 384.0 GB of DDR4 RAM operating at 3 GHz, providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance is equipped with 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong performance in specific workloads. The instance achieves a PassMark CPU score of 48727.93 and an encryption test score of 43605.77, both placing in the top 25% tier. Static web serving throughput is also strong at 42.15 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth tests show 2488.72 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 164.84 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. In database operations, the server processes 44.3 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 21107.91 on PassMark database operations, reflecting average tier performance. For language model tasks, the system processes 7B model prompts at 92.15 tokens per second and generates text at 25.02 tokens per second.

The r6idn.12xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive and high-throughput workloads by combining large DDR4 memory capacity with high-speed local NVMe storage and 75 Gbps network bandwidth. This resource density reduces the need to scale horizontally for memory-bound tasks. However, the lack of hardware GPU acceleration limits its efficiency for heavy deep learning training, though it remains capable of CPU-based LLM inference. This instance is optimized for memory-intensive databases, high-throughput web serving, data caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring fast local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8375C processor. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory, 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 75 Gbps baseline network interface. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance supports AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. Benchmarks show strong performance in CPU encryption and static web serving, alongside solid memory bandwidth. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio and fast local storage make this instance highly efficient for memory-bound workloads without requiring horizontal scaling. It is best suited for large-scale databases, caching layers, high-throughput web serving, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, 2850 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, ideal for databases and caching.

tagline: A memory-optimized, high-bandwidth instance featuring local NVMe storage and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 384 GB DDR4 RAM delivering a 1:8 resource ratio.
2850 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer.
Strong performance in CPU encryption and static web serving benchmarks.
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, caching, and enterprise applications.
